content.problemStatement:
For any array BBB of length MMM, define:


The prefix sum Si=∑j=1iBjS_i = \sum_{j=1}^{i} B_jSi​=∑j=1i​Bj​ for 1≤i≤M1 \le i \le M1≤i≤M.
The value f(B)f(B)f(B) as the number of indices iii such that SiS_iSi​ is a **prime** number.
You are given an array AAA of length NNN, consisting only of the numbers 111 and 222.

Let MMM denote the maximum value of f(B)f(B)f(B) among all arrays BBB that can be obtained by **reordering** the elements of AAA.

You are allowed to perform the following operation multiple times on AAA:


Choose an index iii (1≤i<N1 \le i < N1≤i<N) and swap the adjacent elements AiA_iAi​ and Ai+1A_{i+1}Ai+1​.
Compute the **minimum** number of adjacent swaps required to transform AAA into some array BBB satisfying f(B)=Mf(B) = Mf(B)=M.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN — the number of elements in the array AAA.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers — the elements of the array AAA.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum number of adjacent swaps needed to reach a rearrangement BBB with f(B)=Mf(B) = Mf(B)=M.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 5⋅1035\cdot10^35⋅103.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Consider all reorderings of AAA:

B=[1,2,2]B=[1,2,2]B=[1,2,2], S=[1,3,5]S=[1,3,5]S=[1,3,5], f(B)=2f(B) = 2f(B)=2
B=[2,1,2]B=[2,1,2]B=[2,1,2], S=[2,3,5]S=[2,3,5]S=[2,3,5], f(B)=3f(B) = 3f(B)=3
B=[2,2,1]B=[2,2,1]B=[2,2,1], S=[2,4,5]S=[2,4,5]S=[2,4,5], f(B)=2f(B) = 2f(B)=2

Hence, we know that M=3M = 3M=3, achieved by the reordering B=[2,1,2]B=[2,1,2]B=[2,1,2].
From A=[1,2,2]A=[1,2,2]A=[1,2,2] to [2,1,2][2,1,2][2,1,2]: only a single swap is needed, we swap adjacent elements at positions (1,2)(1,2)(1,2) once.
Test case 222: Initially, A=[1,1,2,2]A=[1,1,2,2]A=[1,1,2,2]

Swap (2,3)(2,3)(2,3): A=[1,2,1,2]A=[1,2,1,2]A=[1,2,1,2]
Swap (1,2)(1,2)(1,2): A=[2,1,1,2]A=[2,1,1,2]A=[2,1,1,2]
Swap (3,4)(3,4)(3,4): A=[2,1,2,1]A=[2,1,2,1]A=[2,1,2,1]

B=[2,1,2,1]B=[2,1,2,1]B=[2,1,2,1], S=[2,3,5,6]S=[2,3,5,6]S=[2,3,5,6], f(B)=3f(B)=3f(B)=3
The number of adjacent swaps required = 333.
It can be shown that f(B)=3f(B)=3f(B)=3 can't be attained with fewer than 333 swaps.
Test case 333: The array AAA already satisfies f(A)=Mf(A)=Mf(A)=M, so no additional swaps are required to satisfy the condition.
